political.s: FEC: $35K PAC donations; 44% to Republican, 56% to Democratic committees.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]

charity.s: $65M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.07% of revenue).

environment.s: CDP A-List (2024) — top tier for climate disclosure and emissions-reduction action.

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture.

privacy.s: CISA Known Exploited Vulns: 2 CVEs on record (1 in past 12mo); affected products include RecoverPoint for Virtual Machines (RP4VMs), dbutil Driver.

wiki.extract: Dell Inc., formerly Dell Computer Corporation, is an American technology company that develops, sells, repairs, and supports personal computers (PCs), servers, data storage devices, network switches, software, computer peripherals including printers and webcams among other products and services. Dell is based in Round Rock, Texas.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Dell RecoverPoint for Virtual Machines (RP4VMs) Use of Hard-coded Credentials Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Dell RecoverPoint for Virtual Machines (RP4VMs) contains an use of hard-coded credentials vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to gain unauthorized access to the underlying operating system and root-level persistence.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Dell dbutil Driver Insufficient Access Control Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Dell dbutil driver contains an insufficient access control vulnerability which may lead to escalation of privileges, denial-of-service (DoS), or information disclosure.

Dell dbutil Driver Insufficient Access Control Vulnerability

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was driving a utility vehicle and making rounds in the facility. While transitioning from the paved portion of the roadway to the uneven gravel portion, the vehicle overturned and the employee's left foot was caught between the vehicle and the ground. The employee sustained a dislocated left ankle.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level  less than 6 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was holding a piece of sheet metal while standing on the second rung from the bottom of a ladder. A gust of wind caused the employee to fall from the ladder to the ground, striking his head. The employee sustained a head injury, lost consciousness, and was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cleaning a conveyor belt used to transport mushrooms. The employee noticed a piece of mushroom that was stuck in the conveyor. He was grabbing the piece when his right hand and arm got stuck in the conveyor, resulting in a laceration. The conveyor belt was energized at the time of the incident.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking to the parking garage when they tripped over a yellow parking block and fell to the ground, sustaining a fractured humerus that required surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by swinging part of powered vehicle
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was removing a chain that was caught between the gate and box of a truck. After the chain was removed, the gate closed, catching the employee's right index and ring fingers between the gate and the box. Both fingertips were amputated.

sampleRecalls.title: Dell Recalls Hybrid Power Adapters Sold with Power Banks Due to Shock Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Dell hybrid power adapters sold with power banks. The adapter is designed to be disconnected from the power bank via release tabs. The recalled adapters were manufactured from January 2017 through March 2017 and can be used separately from the power bank while connected to a power outlet. The adap…
hazards: The hybrid power adapter casing can break and detach, exposing the internal metal components, posing an electric shock hazard.
injuries: Dell has received 11 reports of the hybrid power adapter breaking and exposing the internal components. No injuries have been reported.
products: Hybrid Power Adapters sold with Power Banks
units: About 8,900 (in addition, about 475 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: BabyHome USA Recalls High Chairs Due to Strangulation Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall includes Eat model high chairs in red, black, green, purple, navy, orange, and brown. The model number BH2104 is located on a label on the back of the high chair. The word "babyhome" is printed on one leg of the chair and the word "eat" is printed on the opposite leg of the chair. The high chairs have a ny…
hazards: The front opening between the tray and seat bottom of the high chair can allow a child's body to pass through and become entrapped at the neck. This poses a st…

sampleRecalls.title: Hy Cite Enterprises Recalls Royal Prestige Thermal Wall Pots and Pans Due to Burn and Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves stainless steel 9-ply "Thermal Wall" pans sold under the "Royal Prestige" brand name. The products were sold individually and in sets. The following cookware is included in this recall: 1.5 qt. saucepan 2 qt. saucepan 3 qt. Dutch oven 4 qt. Dutch oven 6 qt. Dutch oven 8 qt. Dutch oven 8" skillet 1…
hazards: The cookware can collapse, crimp or severely deform when exposed to heat, posing a burn and fire hazard to the consumer and nearby property.
injuries: The company is aware of 1,136 reports of cookware collapsing, crimping or deforming, including one report of consumers getting spattered with hot oil when the pan collapsed inward while cooking.

sampleRecalls.title: Swim 'N Score Dive Sticks Recalled by Modell's Due to Risk of Impalement Injury to Children
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled Pool Stix are weighted plastic tubes, which stand upright when tossed into water. The sticks are about 8" long and were sold in packs of 6 in various colors. The UPC (#4969382814) is printed on the product's packaging.
hazards: Children can fall or land on these dive sticks in shallow water and suffer impalement injuries. Eye and other facial injuries are also possible.

sampleRecalls.title: Riddell Recalls Collectible Mini Racing Helmets Due to Violation of Lead Paint Standard
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled product is a six-inch tall, collectible, miniature helmet, modeled after the helmet worn by race car driver Jeff Gordon. The helmet is red and blue with a black visor, and is painted with various sponsor logos and playing cards. The UPC number 095855720029 is located on the bottom of the product's packagi…
hazards: Surface paints on the recalled helmets contain excessive levels of lead, violating the federal lead paint standard.
